articles.headline: 'Finally': Norway star Erling Haaland on fulfilling World Cup dream
articles.description: Erling Haaland tells ESPN he is ready to make his World Cup debut and show his love for Norway on football's biggest stage.

images.name: Erling Haaland [1296x729]
images.caption: Erling Haaland has 55 goals in 50 games for Norway.
images.credit: Jared C. Tilton - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup legends and celebrities reveal their tournament predictions
images.caption: World Cup legends and celebrities reveal their tournament predictions

articles.headline: Jordan Henderson: Luka Modric is the best midfielder I've faced
articles.description: Jordan Henderson has praised Luka Modric as the greatest midfielder he has ever faced, ahead of England's match against Croatia on Wednesday.

images.name: Jordan Henderson Luka Modric [1296x729]
images.alt: Jordan Henderson challenges Luka Modric in the 2018 World Cup semifinal.

images.name: Henderson hails Modric as 'best' midfielder ahead of WC opener
images.caption: Henderson hails Modric as 'best' midfielder ahead of WC opener

articles.headline: World Cup Daily Live: New Zealand's match-predicting puppy poops on live TV
articles.description: Enjoy all the action from Monday's World Cup matches, with features, news, video analysis and color from around the competition.

images.name: Oli Sail, Tova O'Brien and Chris Chang with Bear the dog on TVNZ's Breakfast [1080x608]
Lone Curaçao supporter in Berlin celebrates goal vs. Germany
images.caption: Lone Curaçao supporter in Berlin celebrates goal vs. Germany

articles.headline: World Cup Last Dance: Courtois, Belgium's golden goalkeeper, gets one more go
articles.description: Belgium's 'Golden Generation' were the world's No. 1 team, but didn't win a trophy to show for it. Now, Thibaut Courtois is one of the last ones left.

images.name: Belgium's Thibaut Courtois World Cup 2026 portrait [1296x729]
images.credit: Steph Chambers - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Tielemans: Belgium coming to the World Cup to win
images.caption: Youri Tielemans is targeting World Cup glory as his Belgium side travel to their Seattle base camp.

articles.headline: Rod Stewart pictured at Scotland World Cup win shortly after cancelling concert due to illness
articles.description: Sir Rod Stewart was seen celebrating Scotland's World Cup win over Haiti at Gillette Stadium in Boston on Saturday night shortly after cancelling a concert due to illness.

images.name: Rod Stewart [1296x729]
images.alt: Rod Stewart celebrates at the game between Scotland and Haiti.
images.credit: (Photo by Martin Rickett/PA Images via Getty Images)

images.name: Scotland fans march to Fenway Park with bagpipes in Boston
images.caption: Scotland fans march to Fenway Park with bagpipes in Boston

articles.headline: Explained: How a chip in the ball allowed World Cup's fastest-ever goal by a sub to stand
articles.description: VAR overturned an offside decision after ruling there was a slight flick on the ball by Sweden's Alexander Isak, thanks to some technology in the ball.

images.name: Mattias Svanberg #19 of Sweden scores his team's fourth goal [1296x729]
images.caption: Mattias Svanberg #19 of Sweden scores his team's fourth goal
images.credit: Manuel Velasquez - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

commentary.text: Richard Jolly has the lowdown on England and their issues while Andy Brassell says that the Algerians are ready to represent.
Join us at 1830 GMT to see if England can overcome Algeria and get their World Cup back on track.
Hello all and welcome to live text commentary of England vs Algeria at the Green Point Stadium.
Both of these sides' goalkeepers made mistakes in their respective openers. And both have been punished. James comes in for Green while M'Bolhi comes in for Chaouchi.
Joy Gupta is at the stadium in Cape Town and he will be providing us with some titbits and observations throughout the game.

Comment from Joy Gupta at Green Point Stadium: ''Positively balmy weather - 17 degrees. If England score early they will stroll it. The longer they take to score the more nervy they will become and the tougher Algeria will be to break down.''

In other England team news, Gareth Barry comes in for James Milner. Barry will play in the middle while Steven Gerrard is shifted to left-midfield. Jamie Carragher comes in for the injured Ledley king in the heart of defence.
For Algeria, goalkeeper M'Bolhi is making only his second international appearance, while Rafik Djebbour is replaced by Boudebouz.
The players are out on the pitch and "God Save the Queen" blares out across the Green Point Stadium. Here comes the Algerian national anthem too.
Both teams in need of a positive result after underwhelming openings. England drew 1-1 with USA and Algeria lost 1-0 to Slovenia.
We're just about ready here, the anticipation is tangible.
Comment from Jayaditya Gupta at Green Point stadium: Princes Harry and William are here, just below me. Royalty no less!
We're off..
Algeria kick-off and the North Africans immediately on the attack down the right wing.
The ball goes harmlessly out for a goal kick and it's an early touch for James in goal.
Yahia brings the ball out of defence but he's overhit his pass.
The goalkeepers have had the most touches so far. Probably a good thing to ease their nerves.
Cole overlaps down the left and is brought down by Bougherra.
Free-kick whipped in but Heskey can't direct his header goalwards.
Rooney given the ball on a plate, he passes to Gerrard in acres on the left but he can't make the return ball.
Algeria break at pace and Lacen tries a speculative effort from distance. Blocked away.
Gerrard releases Cole down the left of the penalty area and he turns Belhadj inside out before a good saving tackle is made by Bougherra.
Lacen and Ziani interchange well through the middle and win a corner for Algeria.
Poor delivery from Boudebouz and easily headed away.
Algeria coach Saadane looks a picture of calmness on the touchline, while one of his staff barks instructions enthusiastically.
Barry looks to switch play right to Lennon but it's intercepted well.
Carragher holds his own against Ziani, showing excellent strength against a player who clearly possesses more pace than him.
Lampard and Johnson attempt and exchange but Belhadj does well to force it out.
But his throw-in leaves captain Yahia in trouble and Heskey closes him down quickly.
Gerrard gives the ball away in midfield, he's playing very centrally at the moment.
Punch away from James that was less than convincing with Matmour putting pressure on. Johnson got away with his clearance as well.
Quick break from Lennon results in Heskey winning an England corner.
Gerrard's corner was met by Heskey who headed up and over. He was penalised for a free-kick anyway for using his hands a little heavily.
Cole showing his intent again down the left but this time he can't bring the ball under control.
Heskey fouled by Yahia and England take a quick free-kick - but it's given away immediately. Very poor.
Johnson fouls Ziani and this time the quick free-kick works as Algeria come forward with Boudebbouz. His speculative effort from 25 yards sails wide.
This time it's Gerrard's turn for an optimistic strike. High and over from 35 yards.
M'Bolhi collects the ball to settle his nerves.
Ziani whips a ball in from the left and Johnson almost put it past James. The goalkeepeer keeps hold of it though.
This time it's Belhadj breaking down the left - good pressure here from the Algerians on a nervous looking England defence.
Ziani skips away from Lampard with a smart turn. But the move breaks down.
Carragher across to tackle Matmour in an advanced position down the left flank again for Algeria.
Yebda gets a header in from Ziani's cross but James has it in his hands.
England free-kick as Bougherra brings Gerrard down about 30 yards out.
Short one to Rooney whose shot is deflected into the goalkeeper's hands.
ESPN Stats & Info: In the first 17 minutes, Emile Heskey has had just four touches and one pass.
Bougherra off on a run down the right but Barry gets a challenge in to clear.
Boudebouz has a shot from just inside the area but he slices it wide with his left. Plenty of pressure from Algeria - the green wave keeps on coming.
Great cross from Bougherra and Barry gets in ahead of Yebda to divert it out for a corner.
Belhadj takes it but his delivery is collected very impressively by James.
Boudebouz chases down Cole and James nervously kicks it away. Not a fantastic backpass.
Gareth Barry of England jumps over the tackle by Ryad Boudebouz of Algeria
Lennon has moved to the left to try to get some joy and his first contribution is to win a free-kick.
Barry crosses in but it's over Heskey's head and out for a free-kick in Algeria's right-back position.
Lampard and Barry exchange passes and the ball goes wide to Johnson. Lennon (back on the right) tries to prod it into Heskey but it's a poor ball.
Heskey wins it from Haliche and plays it through to Rooney but he is penalised for being a little rough with Yahia.
ESPN Stats & Info: England's starting line-up today is the second oldest line-up so far at World Cup 2010. Australia had the oldest.
Bougherra crosses and Barry is the man to clear it away again - another great interception.
Terry down on the ground with an apparent head injury.
England fans trying to get their voices to rise above the vuvuzelas.
Great combination between Rooney and Gerrard - crisp interchange. Gerrard shoots from 25 yards but M'Bolhi holds onto it.
Johnson looks for Heskey with a cross but it's headed away.
Rooney starting to brighten up. He escapes into some space and looks for the one-two with Lampard. But Rooney is offside and the pass is overhit.
Belhadj down the left but his cross is met successfully by James.
Algeria certainly doing well thus far but England just starting to build some momentum.
Great chance!
Lennon gets the cross in and it breaks to Lampard who hits a left foot effort. But M'Bolhi again keeps hold of the ball.
Lampard struck it well and it was a smart save to prevent it finding the bottom right corner.
Boudebouz charges through the middle but Gerrard gets a foot in and Lennon advances down the right.
Ziani breaks after the ref waved away Rooney's claims for a foul. He cuts in from the left and strikes a decent effort just wide of James' right post.
Kadir dribbles down the right and Algeria still maintaining a good level of pressure.
But Rooney dispossesses him and suddenly Lennon has the ball on the edge of the box.
It drops to Gerrard on the edge of the area but his ambitious shot could be out of the stadium it went so high in the air.
Heskey holds up James' long ball well and now England advance through the middle.
Lennon picks out Barry but his shot from about 25 yards out is straight down M'Bolhi's throat.
Rooney with a driving run down the left but he is penalised for pushing the back-tracking Kadir.
David James holds onto a ball into the box
Heskey works it down the right and his cross is blocked away. Surely he needs to be the one in the box?!
Ziani has been a real dangerman for the Desert Foxes in this half, unafraid to run at England's defence.
ESPN Stats & Info: Both teams are having trouble crossing the ball. England have put in ten crosses, but only two have found their target, while only two of Algeria's 13 have connected
Rooney has a go from 30 yards but again M'Bolhi is up to the challenge. He's looked very competent considering it's only his second appearance for his country.
It's all petered out a little bit here.
Belhadj trying to make something happen for Algeria, they should definitely be the happier of the two sides at half-time.
HALF-TIME. England 0-0 Algeria
A really tough game to watch from an England perspective, they lack a degree of flair and creativity - Joe Cole anyone?
But fair play to Algeria, they look a much better side than the one that was turned over by Slovenia. Ziani and Boulebouz the key men, while Belhadj has been full of running too.
You can follow the highs and lows of the World Cup on our Facebook page
The other game in Group C was played earlier today and it was a cracker. Slovenia and USA drew 2-2 and Soccernet Editor John Brewin was there to watch it. Read his thoughts on the game here.
Comment from Joy Gupta at Green Point Stadium: Algeria looked the brighter... especially Belhadj and Ziani... but they lack a proper target.
It's like you read my mind Joy!
Fingers crossed for some goals in the second half folks! I thought the World Cup had started to get interesting?!
ESPN Stats & Info: England haven't been able to get the ball in the box for their forwards. Rooney has just two touches in the box and Heskey has one.
Not ideal from your strikers!
Fabio Capello shows his frustration
And we're off for the second half...
England look to attack early on but it's the same old story and the move breaks down before it even reaches the penalty area.
Algeria free-kick as Matmour is sandwiched between Terry and Carragher.
It's a good 35 yards out and it will be captain Yahia to take it.
Straight into Wayne Rooney in the wall.
Great tackle by Johnson on Yebda as he was inside the box.
Heskey headed it on to Rooney who flicked it on for Gerrard but Bougherra was in position to clear it.
Terry penalised for a foul on Matmour and it's another 35-yard free-kick. Yebda this time...
Blocked by Johnson - it was never going anywhere near though.
But Algeria back on the attack immediately with Yebda. His cross goes out of play for a goal-kick though.
Carrick warming up. Can he be the answer to England's prayers?
Heskey sprints down the right - I use the word sprint loosely - before spooning his cross out of play.
Robin at Soccernet HQ says:When England played Egypt, Carrick came on and changed the game - he could do it again.
Not sure about that one Robin!
Ziani fouls Johnson - he's unlucky there though as it was actually Johnson who kicked his foot.
Barry looking for a killer pass but he's given it away and is fortunate that Algeria don't manufacture a break.
Gerrard fed in by Lampard on the right of the area and he's in acres. He tries to drop it back in the box but it's poor and cleared for a corner.
The ball comes out to Lennon who strikes one with his left from the edge of the box. Tame effort to say the least.
Boulabouz and Matmour make some space but it's a break for England.
But now Algeria back on the attach with Belhadj. It's end to end stuff.
Cole heads Belhadj's cross out for an Algeria corner.
Belhadj to swing it in.
Short to Ziani and his cross is cleared by Heskey.
Carragher booked and will miss the next game.
Free-kick 20 yards out on the left of the area. Yahia to take it for Algeria...
Dragged wide of the left post, his low effort skewed out for a goal-kick.
Wayne Rooney looks frustrated
England corner as Rooney continues to look dismayed. He has shown some really poor touches in the last ten minutes.
Gerrard delivers but M'Bolhi collects.
Cole charges down Kadir and Algeria have a throw-in deep in England territory.
Lampard wins it back and Rooney can break. He switches it to Johnson.
Lennon down the right and he floats a great ball in towards rooney - flicked away by Halliche for a corner.
Wright-Phillips on for Lennon
Gerrard's ball is flapped at by M'Bolhi but it's hammered upfield.
Terrible back pass there by Terry, criminal in fact.
James does really well to get it away. Could have been disaster with Matmour bearing down on him.
Soccernet Editor John Brewin: As a sole Englishman in a press room of Americans and Slovenians I am utterly embarrased by this performance.
Another dire touch from Rooney, he has had a real nightmare.
Great positive running from Wright-Phillips. He's won a free-kcik down the right wing. Barry to swing it in.
It comes to Lampard who tries but - surprise surprise - fails to execute the pass.
James and Carragher looked unsure who was to come for the ball. In the end Carragher hoofed it out.
Heskey doing some desperate defeding in hs own area.
ESPN Stats & Info: Aaron Lennon had just 30 touches before coming off, the least amount for any player on England and the third least for either team.
Rooney with another loose pass, this time too short for Gerrard.
ESPN Stats & Info: Algeria have never kept a clean sheet in seven World Cup matches.
Matmour takes on Carragher down the left but the Liverpool defender makes a good tackle.
Great chance for England
Heskey into the box tried to cross it but his effort was deflected away by Belhadj with a last-ditch tackle. It dipped just over the bar.
The subsequent corner was met by Gerrard who headed into the goalkeeper's arms.
Ziani on the attack for Algeria, he sends Matmour clear down the left but he's dispossessed and Capello's side have it back.
Defoe warming up.
Rooney let's fly from all of 30 yards. Wide.
Abdoun on for Boulebouz
Defoe on for Heskey
Interesting to see how that much-maligned partnership between Rooney and Defoe will worlk out.
Great build up between Gerrard and Lampard. Defoe bursts through and wins a corner. Good first contribution.
Terrible delivery by Gerrard.
Another good burst of pace from Defoe and he's forced another corner.
Lampard to take.
Terry nods it to Barry on the edge who hammers his shot into Halliche's arm. Completely accidental though.
Great acceleration from Belhadj - he's been effective throughout. But the one-two with Ziani doesn't quite work.
England corner, won again by Defoe. But no further developments to report after that.
Terry tries to bring England forward from the back.
Wright-Phillips tries to slip it through to Gerrard but his pass is too strong.
Guedioura on for Ziani
Ziani has been impressive but looked to be suffering from a bit of cramp.
Matmour tackled fairly by Johnson but when the ball comes to Rooney he again can't create anything.
Wright-Phillips wins the free-kick - he's looked lively since coming on.
Johnson's cross is too long.
Lampard tries to engineer some space on the edge of the box but after giving it to Johnson he watches as the right-back fails to deliver.
Crouch on for Barry
Rooney dropping back to left midfield by the looks of it. Interesting tactical switch.
Lacen cautioned for bringing down Wright-Phillips.
England corner delivered towards Defoe. Goal kick as he heads it over.
Comment from Joy Gupta at Green Point Stadium: Flares going off in the stands now. It's coming from the Algerian part - I think they sense a point they didn't expect.
Rooney with so much space oin front of him looks for the Hollywood pass to Wright-Phillips and fails miserably.
Lampard shoots from 30 yards. It's all looking very desperate as boos ring out, seemingly from both sets of fans. Capello looks irate.
Mesbah on for Yebda
Frank Lampard shows his frustration after a missed chance
Terry penalised and Algeria have a free-kick about 40 yards out.
Belhadj with a fantastic ball, very dangerous...
James collects. If he didn't catch it, there were three players in green ready to pounce. Crucial from the goalkeeper.
Three minutes of added time to play.
Defoe and Gerrard combine well, Lampard is slipped in and the ball breaks out for Defoe who launches it high and over. Don't think the Jabulani can be blamed for that.
Crouch with an excellent tiptoeing run down the right but his ball in field is terrible.
Johnson brings down Belhadj.
Rooney fouls Kadir - one last chance for Algeria.
The box is packed with green shirts, Belhadj to take.
Sails over the crossbar.
Late corner for England.
It's all over...
FULL-TIME. England 0-0 Algeria
By no means a classic this one and I'm sure Fabio Capello will be doing a lot of soul searching before the Slovenia game.
Soccernet Editor John Brewin: That was the worst match of the tournament and England can take a huge part of the discredit for it. As bad as 1-1 with Ireland in 1990, Denmark in 1992. Plain awful.
Comment from Joy Gupta at Green Point Stadium: Flares going off in the stands now. It's coming from the Algerian part - I think they sense a point they didn't expect.
And they got it - a great performance really from Algeria - things slowed down for them in the second half but they were the better side in the first.
You can read the report from this game here.
You can follow the highs and lows of the World Cup on our Facebook page

Comment from Joy Gupta: Algeria fully deserve their point and they will be celebrating back home long into the night. They worked hard and kept pressing till the end. What they needed was a finisher but strangely they didn't bring either any of their forwards...
Gerrard was just asked what was missing from the game. "Goals" was his answer. Insightful.
Right folks, that's all for today but join us again on Saturday for coverage of Cameroon v Denmark, Netherlands v Japan, Ghana v Australia. Thanks for tuning in.
